incident_summary: API gateway experienced severe latency degradation (p99 15.2s vs baseline ~500ms) for approximately 2.5 hours due to database connection pool exhaustion triggered by a configuration change that tripled HTTP client timeout values.

root_cause.summary: Configuration change increased HTTP client timeout from 10s to 30s, causing database connections to be held 3x longer and exhausting the fixed-size connection pool (50 connections).
root_cause.trigger: Deployment at 2025-01-15T14:15:00Z changing HTTP client timeout configuration from 10s to 30s
root_cause.mechanism: Longer timeout values caused database connections to remain active for extended periods. With a fixed pool size of 50 connections, the pool could not recycle connections fast enough to serve incoming request volume. Once exhausted, new requests waited up to 30 seconds for available connections, cascading into severe API latency.

customer_impact.description: Customers experienced significantly slower API response times, with requests taking up to 15+ seconds instead of the normal sub-second response. Some requests may have timed out entirely if client-side timeouts were shorter than 15 seconds.

resolution.action_taken: Rolled back the configuration change by reverting HTTP client timeout from 30s back to 10s at 2025-01-15T15:45:00Z
resolution.confirmed_by: Connection pool utilization dropped to 60% (from 100%), p99 latency returned to baseline ~500ms by 16:00 UTC, CloudWatch logs showed 'Database connection pool recovered' at 15:05 UTC

root_cause: Slack thread engineer confirmation 'Found it! PR #12345... Changed HTTP client timeout from 10s to 30s'
Slack explanation 'Connection pool can't recycle fast enough with the longer timeout'
Rollback deployment description 'Emergency rollback... due to database connection pool exhaustion'
customer_impact: CloudWatch logs showing 30-second connection wait times
Slack engineer assessment of cascading timeouts

data_gaps: Exact customer request failure rate unknown - only latency metrics available, unclear how many requests actually failed vs. succeeded slowly
Geographic/regional scope unconfirmed - data does not indicate whether all regions were affected or only specific deployments
Baseline request volume unknown - cannot determine if traffic spike contributed or if purely configuration-driven
Recovery timing ambiguous - CloudWatch shows pool recovery at 15:05 UTC but rollback deployed at 15:45 UTC, suggesting partial self-recovery before mitigation
Customer-facing product names unknown - only 'api-gateway' service identified, unclear which specific SaaS products depend on this API
